target: WIN-053 detail_evidence — strengthen the directional/polarity argument in paragraph 1 with explicit monopole-pair-vs-dipole topological framing and Maxwell ∇·B = 0 grounding. Addresses ISS-1245 (WIN-053.c5 H7, still open from c4 H7 / c3 H5).
review_class_override_note: Tracker classified as deep-attack (baby-orphan default, class_hint=deep-attack). Authored EXP declares verification: the directional/polarity argument is ALREADY present in the existing detail_evidence at two locations (para 1 end: "adding a directional contradiction on top of the magnitude failure"; para 3 (ii): "the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair"). The new content is (a) explicit "monopole pair, not a dipole" terminology and (b) a third Maxwell ∇·B = 0 citation framed as the constitutional ban on isolated magnetic charges — both refinements to an existing argument rather than a new line of attack. Per PROP-025: "small additions to existing arguments" → verification (batchable). Override noted here for curmudgeon scheduling transparency.
scope_summary: Single-sentence replacement in data/wins.json WIN-053.detail_evidence, paragraph 1 (the flux-conservation paragraph). Replaces the existing one-sentence directional callout with a three-sentence mini-argument that (a) names the topological form (monopole pair, not a dipole) using the explicit algebraic expression of B(r), (b) cites Maxwell ∇·B = 0 as the constitutional ban on isolated magnetic charges requiring closed-loop fields to reverse sign somewhere, and (c) closes with an "independent topological failure" framing that makes the polarity violation self-supporting even if the magnitude flux-conservation argument were waived. No verdict change. Net add: ~80 words. Closes the last open curmudgeon hole on WIN-053.c5 (H1-H6, H8-H9 already addressed in current text; H7 was the lone remaining item per current-text audit).
kernel_of_truth: The dome's fitted B(r) = A·e^(-r_N/λ) + C·e^(-r_S/λ) does produce a two-peaked surface field that matches observed magnetic-pole magnitudes — kernel: as a phenomenological surface fit to WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole strengths, the two-exponential form is locally accurate within its 20% RMS budget. The dome correctly identifies that the geographic poles both register strong field magnitudes (~55,000 nT) and writes a functional form that captures both peaks. But that purely magnitude-based fit ignores polarity: a closed magnetic circuit requires opposite vector orientations at the two apertures (the field must enter at one pole and exit at the other for flux to balance around the loop). Encoding both apertures as positive-magnitude flux sources reproduces the surface magnitudes while abandoning the closed-circuit topology that motivated the toroidal architecture in the first place. The dome cannot have both: either B(r) is a closed-circuit derivation (and the same-sign coefficients violate ∇·B = 0) or it is a phenomenological surface fit (and the toroidal topology adds no predictive content beyond "two peaks at the poles"). The kernel does not save the claim — it just locates the failure precisely.

patches.target_locator: WIN-053.detail_evidence — paragraph 1 (the flux-conservation paragraph), the directional-constraint sentence near the end of the paragraph. Unique-substring locator confirmed (1 match) per pre-flight check.
patches.current_text: The topology also imposes a directional constraint: with the north pole as flux source and the Antarctic return as flux sink, the field at the southern terminus should be directed oppositely to the northern one; B(r)'s positive coefficient C = 64,852 nT assigns the same outward sign at both ends, adding a directional contradiction on top of the magnitude failure.
patches.proposed_text: The topology also imposes a directional constraint: with the north pole as flux source and the Antarctic return as flux sink, the field at the southern terminus should be directed oppositely to the northern one, yet B(r) = A·e−rN/λ + C·e−rS/λ with A, C both positive is the functional form of a monopole pair — two flux sources of the same sign — not a dipole (source paired with sink). Maxwell's ∇·B = 0 prohibits isolated magnetic charges and therefore requires every closed-loop magnetic field to reverse sign somewhere between its poles; the dome's fit never does. The dome's formula is not merely off by a factor of ~1,660 — in its algebraic form it is the field of two same-signed magnetic monopoles rather than of a closed dipolar circuit, an independent topological failure that survives even if the magnitude discrepancy is set aside.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon WIN-053.c5 Hole 7 (minor, still open from c4 H7 / c3 H5). The existing directional sentence carries the substantive point but lacks (a) the topological terminology ("monopole pair, not a dipole") the curmudgeon recommended and (b) the Maxwell ∇·B = 0 constitutional grounding. Adding these strengthens the polarity argument to stand as an independent failure mode rather than a derivative of the magnitude argument. The expanded sentence preserves the existing structure (source/sink framing, positive-coefficient observation) and adds the algebraic form B(r) = A·e^(-r_N/λ) + C·e^(-r_S/λ) so the structural claim is checkable, then cites ∇·B = 0 explicitly to ground the "must reverse sign" requirement. The closing sentence ("not merely off by a factor of ~1,660") makes the independence-of-magnitude point that the curmudgeon's stronger-arguments section called the most decisive single-sentence rebuttal. Maintains paragraph cohesion and reading flow.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The argumentative thrust is unchanged: directional contradiction added on top of magnitude contradiction. The new content adds terminology and citation grounding to an argument that was already present at two locations in the section (para 1 directional callout, para 3 (ii) closing of the second-aperture rescue). No numerical claims change. The "monopole pair" terminology is technically precise (∇·B = 0 forbids isolated magnetic monopoles; B(r) with two same-sign exponentials is the algebraic form of two such monopoles regardless of physical realizability). No new external sources cited (Maxwell ∇·B = 0 is textbook; Jackson/Griffiths could be optionally added but the equation alone is sufficient for prose). No verdict change on WIN-053.

holes_addressed: WIN-053.c5 H7 (minor): "STILL OPEN — flux direction (source vs sink) not discussed (c4 H7 / c3 H5)" — closed by explicit monopole-pair-vs-dipole topological framing and Maxwell ∇·B = 0 citation.
new_evidence_added: Maxwell ∇·B = 0 cited as the constitutional ban on isolated magnetic charges (third in-text invocation, but first as the "must reverse sign" structural requirement rather than as the flux-magnitude-conservation requirement).
Explicit algebraic form B(r) = A·e^(-r_N/λ) + C·e^(-r_S/λ) brought into paragraph 1 (previously the algebraic form was only spelled out in paragraph 5 and the c4-c5 review back-and-forth).

anticipated_objections.objection: B(r) gives field MAGNITUDE in nT, not signed vector components — so the "positive coefficients" claim is a category error: of course magnitudes are positive.
anticipated_objections.response: Granted that B(r) reads as a magnitude in the dome's prose. But the dome's claim is that the formula DESCRIBES a closed toroidal circuit, which requires the field VECTOR to satisfy ∇·B = 0 at every point — and the vector at the southern aperture cannot point outward (parallel to the north-pole field) without violating flux conservation around the closed loop. The dome owes us either (a) an explicit vector-sign convention in B(r) where C carries a negative sign at the south pole (which the published formula does not have), or (b) an explanation of how the toroidal circuit closes with same-sign vector orientation at both apertures. Re-interpreting B(r) as a magnitude-only fit detaches the equation from the closed-circuit topology — exactly the move our paragraph 2 "narrow channel" defense analysis already closes.

anticipated_objections.objection: A dipole in spherical-harmonic decomposition (degree ℓ=1) does have a real-valued surface intensity with a single sign-changing component. The dome's fit is a magnitude scalar, not a vector — comparing it to ∇·B = 0 requires reconstructing the vector field, which we have not done.
anticipated_objections.response: True — we have not reconstructed the vector field from the dome's scalar B(r). But the dome itself claims the toroidal architecture and uses flux-conservation language for the closed circuit (the "Sump" return). Once you commit to that architecture, the vector orientation at each aperture is fixed by the closed-loop topology, and the surface MAGNITUDE at the southern aperture is constrained by the area ratio (our 1,660:1 calculation). So the polarity argument operates BENEATH the dome's own architectural commitments: even if we never reconstruct the full vector field, the algebraic form of B(r) with two positive exponentials cannot describe the field magnitude of a closed dipolar circuit whose southern surface MUST register either ~39 nT (if the south is the closed-circuit return aperture) or zero/negative net flux (if we treat "sign" as the dome implicitly does in PRED-TOROID-002 magnitude-only language).
